strap: Greater Glasgow Net Zero draws £1.07bn of UKRI lead-led funding across 4,211 grants, anchored by Glasgow (29%), Strathclyde (20%), with Nitech Solutions on the industrial side. 23 Companies House-traced spin-outs translate to £47m UKRI per spin-out.
behaviour: The cluster shows low-confidence "Coordinating instead of deciding" behaviour — multi-actor coordination defers strategic option-collapse, with formal coordination bodies and partnerships absorbing the load that would otherwise force a decisive pathway. Pipeline 1 evidence: planning frameworks and multiple 2024-2025 partnerships are present but Y-side strategic-commitment patterns are absent; the £1.07bn UKRI footprint is heavily Strathclyde-Glasgow led (49% combined) with NItech Solutions (12.8%) the largest industrial anchor.
